evidence_quotes.quote: companies making it hard to unsubscribe.
There should be added when using a subscription model on a service like how Adobe is doing.
There shouldn't be a cancellation for stopping a subscription early.
There should be clear instructions that if there is a cancellation fee, the user should be well informed about it before buying into it.
This should also extend to game publishers "killing" games after end of support.
There should be protective measures from eu to make publishers make a way for the owners of the game to keep being able to play the games that they bought.
It feels predatory to surprise you with a hidden fee like that.
If I buy album CD, there shouldn't be a option for publishers to remove said CD from owners or bricking it.
This is a clear violation of the spirit of owning something.

paragraphs.text: Hello! I'm happy to see the EU taking active measures to secure consumers rights and protect their experience. I see that you have mentioned companies making it hard to unsubscribe. There should be added when using a subscription model on a service like how Adobe is doing. There shouldn't be a cancellation for stopping a subscription early. It feels predatory to surprise you with a hidden fee like that. There should be clear instructions that if there is a cancellation fee, the user should be well informed about it before buying into it. This should also extend to game publishers "killing" games after end of support. This is a clear violation of the spirit of owning something. If I buy album CD, there shouldn't be a option for publishers to remove said CD from owners or bricking it. Same should go for videogames. As there is less and less of a physical market for games and it's moving more to the digital side there should be protective measures from eu to make publishers make a way for the owners of the game to keep being able to play the games that they bought. There is already an inititive called Stop Killing Games which has over 1 million signatures that is working for this. I mean there is games that I own like Dishonored that i'm afraid that I might not be able to play in the future. Please look into this inititive and take inspiration from it to this Act. Thank you
